senses.definition: to remove or set something apart and isolate it, often to protect or preserve it or to keep it away from others
examples: The court ordered the funds to be sequestered during the investigation.
The prisoner was sequestered from the main group to ensure safety.

senses.definition: plural of sequestrum or a plural form referring to a series of sequestra (anatomical or botanical term rarely used outside specialist contexts)
examples: The fossil displayed several sequestra along the specimen.
In anatomy texts, sequestra may describe separated bone fragments.

senses.definition: capable of being sequestrated or removed and isolated
examples: The assets were deemed sequestrable by the court.
Sequestrable funds can be frozen during legal proceedings.

senses.definition: relating to sequestration or the act of sequestering; involving isolation or setting apart
examples: Sequestral remedies were discussed in the legal brief.
A sequestral approach was taken to protect the artifacts.

senses.definition: a substance that sequesters or binds substances to prevent reactions, often used in chemistry or medicine
examples: The sequestrant helped prevent metal ion interference in the solution.
Food additives can act as sequestrants to preserve color.

senses.definition: multiple substances that sequester or bind to other compounds to inhibit reactions or bind ions
examples: Examples of sequestrants include EDTA and Citric Acid as food preservatives.
Dietary supplements may include sequestrants to reduce mineral interactions.

senses.definition: to sequester; to take legal possession of property until a debt or claim is resolved
examples: The court decided to sequestrate the debtor’s assets.
Authorities can sequestrate funds to secure repayment.

senses.definition: having been sequestrated; having assets or property taken into custody or isolation
examples: The court-sequestrated funds were later released.
The seized assets were sequestrated for the duration of the case.

senses.definition: third person singular present of sequestrate; to sequester
examples: The court sequestrates assets when disputes arise.
She sequestrates funds to prevent improper use.

senses.definition: the act of sequestrating; the process of setting apart or confiscating
examples: Authorities are sequestrating the disputed assets.
Sequestrating funds can delay payment.

senses.definition: the act of sequestration; the legal process of taking custody of assets or the state of being separated or isolated
examples: Sequestration of assets occurred during the bankruptcy proceedings.
The sequestration of the animal’s habitat protected it from harm.

senses.definition: plural of sequestration; instances of sequestration or measures of sequestration
examples: Several sequestrations were reported in the case.
Tax sequestrations reduced available revenue.

senses.definition: a person who sequestrates; an official who seizes or isolates assets or property
examples: The sequestrator was cited for improper handling of funds.
Officials acted as sequestrators in the case.

senses.definition: multiple sequestrators; people or officials who sequester assets or property
examples: The sequestrators placed holds on the funds.
Sequestrators must follow strict legal procedures.

senses.definition: feminine plural form of sequestratrix; female sequestrators
examples: The sequestratrices oversaw the asset withdrawals.
Historically, sequestratrices played roles in property disputes.

senses.definition: a female sequestrator; a person who sequestrates assets or property
examples: The sequestratrix filed the necessary documents.
As a sequestratrix, she managed the confiscated funds.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure to remove sequestra (dead bone fragments) from bone
examples: The patient underwent sequestrectomy to treat osteomyelitis.
Postoperative imaging checked for complete sequestrum removal after sequestrectomy.

senses.definition: a surgical operation to cut out sequestra from bone
examples: Surgeons performed a sequestrotomy to remove infected bone.
Successful sequestrotomy reduced infection risk.

senses.definition: a piece of dead bone that has become separated from healthy bone due to infection or injury
examples: X-rays showed a sequestrum in the jaw bone.
Treatment may involve removing the sequestrum to allow healing.

senses.definition: plural of sequestrum; multiple pieces of dead bone
examples: The X-ray revealed several sequestrums in the affected area.
Treatment involved removing the sequestrums.

senses.definition: a small shiny decoration sewn onto clothing, usually a flat disk with a hole in the middle
examples: Her dress was covered with silver sequins.
The costume sparkled with colorful sequins.

senses.definition: decorated with sequins
examples: She wore a sequined top that shimmered in the light.
The sequined gown looked festive and bright.

senses.definition: the act of decorating with sequins
examples: She is sequencing the fabric for the costume.
They are sequining the costumes for the show.

senses.definition: decorated with sequins
examples: A sequinned dress glittered under the stage lights.
She wore a sequinned scarf to the party.

senses.definition: tiny shining decorative pieces sewn onto clothing
examples: The outfit was covered in blue sequins.
She added sequins to the costume for extra sparkle.
